calendar.matters:
Petition To Recover Possession Of Trust Real Property, For Ejectment Of Unlawful Occupants, And Instructing Trustees To Sell Property
Off Calendar Sep-17-2019 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Adam Lazar for Irene Benack and Margaret McCormick (Petitioners).
Kellyann Estrem for Theresa Dune (Respondent).
Brian Connors for Frances Rivette (Respondent).

Mr. Lazar stated that they settled and have a signed agreement, that the intent is to move forward on the terms of the settlement, that Theresa will have until the end of the year to purchase the home, that if not, Theresa will have to move by the end of March and that his Clients are suggesting a continuance for a status hearing. The Court noted that the settlement agreement establishes a relationship, that it resolves this petition and that if it breaks apart, the Parties will need to file a new petition. The Petition to Recover Possession of Trust Real Property, etc. is off calendar.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

Petition To Recover Possession Of Trust Real Property, For Ejectment Of Unlawful Occupants, And Instructing Trustees To Sell Property
Off Calendar Aug-06-2019 Continued To Sep-17-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Margaret McCormick for Irene F. Benack (Petitioner). Brian Connors, Evan Winet

Mr. Winet requested a short continuance stating that some progress has been made, that there has been a plury of activity, that Teresa has added some comments to the settlement agreement, but they do not have a signed settlement yet and that there are some issues to work out. The Amended Petition to Recover Possession of Trust Real Property; for Ejectment of Unlawful Occupants; for Removal of Co-Trustees; and Instructing Trustee to Sell Property is continued to September 17, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. A joint status statement is to be filed by September 10, 2019.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: B. Wan. Not reported.

Petition To Recover Possession Of Trust Real Property, For Ejectment Of Unlawful Occupants, And Instructing Trustees To Sell Property
Off Calendar Jul-09-2019 Continued To Aug-06-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Evan Winet for and with Irene Benack and Margie McCormick (Beneficiaries).
Brian Connors for Frances Rivette (Respondent).
Vernon Goins for Theresa Dunne (Respondent).
Janet Terpening and Julia Dunne also present.

The Court informed the Parties that it is ready to proceed on the issue of the instructions and removal of the trustee, but not on the real estate issue. Mr. Winet stated that they have had discussions, that things are moving a little slow, but they have been in conversation, and that the parties agree that the house needs to be appraised and that Theresa wants to buy out the other siblings. Mr. Winet also stated that the question is if Theresa will be capable of a buy out and that they are asking for a short continuance to work on settlement and for Theresa to obtain approval on a loan, or move out if she is unable to qualify. Mr. Goins stated that he believes they have agreement on the basics and common material, that he presented a proposed settlement, that he had been out of the office and that he is part of the delay. The Petition to Recover Possession of Trust Real Property, etc. is continued to August 6, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Parties are to provide a joint status statement as to where things stand by July 30, 2019.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Reporter: Angela Pourtabib, CSR #13714, 855-348-4997, calendar@firstlegalnetwork.com

Petition To Recover Possession Of Trust Real Property, For Ejectment Of Unlawful Occupants, And Instructing Trustees To Sell Property
Off Calendar Jun-04-2019 Continued To Jul-09-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Evan Winet for and with Irene Benack (by telephone) and for Margaret McCormick (Petitioners).
Brian Connors for Frances Rivette (Respondent).
Vernon Goins for Theresa Dunne (Respondent).

The Court informed the parties that the issue of removal of the Co-Trustees and the request to sell the property are properly before this Court, that it will not hear the ejectment proceeding, that there is a special Housing Court, that the ejectment/unlawful detainer action needs to be taken to Dept. 501 and that it will not be heard in the Probate Court. Mr. Winet stated that the parties have had discussion, that there is no dispute that the trust owns the property and should be distributed six ways, that the dispute is how and when, and that there are some factual disputes. Mr. Winet also stated that Theresa is interested in purchasing the property from the other heirs, that there is some concern as to whether she can do this, that they need an appraisal and that the sale should be for the current value of the property. In addition, Mr. Winet asked for a short continuance to early July to see if Theresa can come up with something and to see if the parties can work out a settlement agreement. The Petition to Recover Possession of Trust Real Property, etc. is continued to July 9, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court expects the parties to do what they can to try and get this resolved, that the parties talk and stated that if they need help, they can come back and ask for a Mediator.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

Petition To Recover Possession Of Trust Real Property, For Ejectment Of Unlawful Occupants, And Instructing Trustees To Sell Property
